title: USD Stage Population Contract

included: populate a stage hierarchy from a NamespaceSource
optional population mask with ancestor inclusion
prim traversal order using primOrder over source child names
property traversal order using strongest/source propertyOrder over source property names
preservation of provider-composed child/property ordering when declared by the NamespaceSource
default prim lookup from the root layer field
prim query flags for active, loaded, defined, abstract, and instanceable when source opinions are available
portable model hierarchy query and traversal for kind values group, assembly, component, and subcomponent
instance summaries for instanceable prims with composition-arc provenance supplied by the consumed namespace source
shared representation identifiers for matching instanceable prims with identical supported composition-arc provenance and compatible population-mask context
arc-only descendant population for supported non-local composition opinions below instanceable prims when the consumed composed namespace source supplies instance-filtered inputs
authored attribute and relationship enumeration with typed source-visible field values from the namespace source
schema-defined property enumeration when the consumed namespace source declares schema_fallbacks present
diagnostics passthrough from the namespace source
deferred: composition arc evaluation when the namespace source declares composition absent
computing schema fallback properties locally instead of consuming them from a namespace source
portable custom aliases and validation diagnostics for model hierarchy kind values
implementation-specific prototype query APIs not required by the pinned AOUSD instancing text
payload load/unload policy when the namespace source declares payload loading absent

model.Stage: Target-native composed scene graph view populated from a NamespaceSource. It is an immutable query surface after construction, not a document-model Layer and not a JSON object.
model.SceneObject: Populated prim-like stage node at / or at an absolute prim path.
model.PopulationMask: Optional set of absolute prim paths. Matching paths and their ancestors are included; unrelated paths are not populated.
model.PrimIndex: Implementation-owned lookup/index data for populated prims. This contract does not require the OpenUSD PCP data structure.
model.PopulationSource: The NamespaceSource consumed by this skill. Source capability declarations determine which composition/value-resolution features the populated stage may claim.
model.ModelHierarchyState: A query predicate and traversal subset derived from resolved kind tokens. Portable values are group, assembly, component, and subcomponent only.
model.InstanceState: A query record for a populated prim marked instanceable, including whether a composition-backed instance arc signature was available and whether a shared representation was assigned.
model.SharedRepresentation: Implementation-modeled identity for a populated instance subtree. This contract uses stable IDs for matching supported composition-arc provenance under compatible population masks.
model.FallbackPolicy: Stage population applies population-query fallbacks for active=true, loaded=true when payload loading is absent, instanceable=false, empty kind/typeName/defaultPrim, specifier=over when missing.

input.populationMask: optional list of absolute prim paths
input.payloadInclusion: optional adapter-only payload inclusion scenario; ignored only when source declares payload loading absent

output.defaultPrim: populated default prim path or empty string

output.paths: ordered populated prim paths that are part of the portable contiguous model hierarchy, excluding / and excluding descendants of component prims

output.name: final prim name
output.typeName: resolved/population field token spelling or empty string
output.specifier: def, over, or class with fallback policy applied
output.active: stage query flag including ancestor activity

output.kind: resolved/population kind token spelling or empty string
output.modelHierarchy: whether the prim is part of the portable contiguous model hierarchy
output.modelHierarchyContinuity: whether descendants may continue the model hierarchy through this prim
output.modelHierarchyTerminal: whether this prim is a component that is included but terminates model hierarchy traversal

output.instanceable: source-visible instanceable opinion with fallback false
output.instance: true only when instanceable is true and the consumed composed namespace source supplies supported composition-arc provenance for the prim
output.hasInstanceArcs: whether supported instance-source composition arcs contributed to the prim
output.sharedRepresentationId: implementation-owned shared representation identifier, empty when none is assigned
output.sharedRepresentationFirstUse: whether this prim populated the shared representation in this stage

output.name: property name
output.fields: authored or source-visible typed field map

laws: stage population must consume a NamespaceSource rather than reading parser-local USDA structures
stage population must not claim composition semantics beyond the source capability declaration
the populated Stage is target-native and must not be represented internally as canonical dump JSON
stage query indexes must retain paths, property names, child names, typeName/kind tokens, and field keys as semantic runtime types rather than raw strings

public Stage APIs must not expose mutable owned population indexes, path-to-prim maps, path-to-property maps, traversal arrays, or ordering caches; any mutation needed during construction must be private to the population builder
the absolute root path is always the root scene object, but traversal results exposed to user-facing prim iteration exclude / unless an adapter explicitly asks for root
active=false on a prim excludes that prim's descendants from population; an inactive prim itself is not included in the traversable stage unless a future contract explicitly adds inactive querying
population masks include requested prims and ancestors of requested prims, and exclude unrelated branches
a population mask path under an inactive ancestor does not force inactive descendants to populate
prim traversal uses source child names reordered by primOrder; names not listed in primOrder retain source child order for a single-layer source
property traversal sorts source property names by path element ordering, then applies the strongest/source propertyOrder sparse ordering
when a namespace source declares child_ordering=composed or property_ordering=composed, stage population preserves the provider order and must not reapply single-layer ordering rules
defaultPrim resolves to a populated root child with the named token; missing, empty, or masked-out default prim returns an empty defaultPrim result
defined is true when the prim and all populated ancestors have specifier def or class; abstract is true when the prim or any populated ancestor has specifier class
loaded is true for populated prims when the source declares payload loading absent; future payload-aware sources must expose load state
instanceable is the source-visible instanceable opinion with fallback false
an instance summary may claim instance=true only when the prim is marked instanceable and the consumed composed namespace source supplies supported composition-arc provenance for the prim

supported shared instance representations use the strongest-to-weakest non-local composition-arc provenance supplied by the composition source plus the relative population-mask context as their matching key
two instanceable prims with matching arc provenance must not share a representation when the population mask affects their subtrees differently

when a supported shared representation has already been populated in the stage, later matching instance prims keep their own root prim but do not repopulate duplicate descendants into the stage traversal

when the consumed composed namespace source supplies instance-filtered descendant inputs, local descendant opinions under an instanceable prim are excluded from populated descendants unless they came from supported composition arcs authored on the instanceable prim or recursively from the source of such arcs

model hierarchy traversal uses only portable kind values: group and assembly are included and continue traversal, component is included and terminates traversal, and subcomponent, empty, or unknown kind values are excluded and terminate continuity
custom kind aliases and validation diagnostics are implementation-dependent and are not portable in this contract

attribute and relationship field maps exposed through property_summary are source-visible fields with preserved typed-read semantics; when the consumed source declares schema_fallbacks present, these maps may include schema-defined fallback fields that must remain distinguishable at the source boundary from authored opinions
diagnostics from layer opening or source construction are preserved and exposed through the stage summary

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with a canonical layer object, optional populationMask, and ops array. The adapter must build target-native document-model and namespace-source objects before calling stage population.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and results.
